bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid. The opponent’s opening offer gives me only moderate utility, and with no messages or concession pattern yet, I will anchor on my strongest values across the high-weight issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.02 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s second offer is actually worse for my utility than their first, so I do not read meaningful concession from them; I will only concede on low-weight issueD, where they have consistently proposed valueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.93 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers have not improved for me; in fact, their utility to me has declined from about 0.44 to 0.35. Since they have repeatedly offered issueD: valueC and issueB: valueB, I am only making a small concession on the low-weight issueD while holding firm on the most valuable issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.06 and the opponent’s valid offers remain low for me, around 0.35–0.44 utility, without a clear upward concession trend. The opponent consistently offers issueD: valueC, which is my lowest-weight issue, so I concede there while holding firm on the high-weight issues A, B, and C.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.88 because it is still very early in the negotiation, so there is little need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s offers remain low for my utility and show no clear upward concession trend, but they consistently choose issueD:valueC, so I concede that low-weight issue while keeping my highest-weight issues strong.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for myself while making a low-cost concession on issueD. The opponent has consistently offered issueD:valueC and has not shown meaningful concession on the higher-weight issues, so I will hold firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC while offering their apparent preference on issueD.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for myself while avoiding an exact repeat of my last offer. The opponent’s valid offers have not shown meaningful concession and consistently use issueD=valueC, which is a low-weight concession for me, so I keep my strongest positions on issueA and issueB while offering a moderate alternative on issueC and matching issueD=valueC.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid for myself because it is still early in the negotiation at t=0.14, so there is little need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s valid offers have stayed low-value for me and show no clear concession trend, so I am holding firm on high-weight issues while only conceding issueD, which is least important to me.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid rather than making major concessions. The opponent’s offers have stayed around low utility for me and do not show meaningful concession, though they repeatedly choose issueD=valueC; since issueD is low weight, I can vary it later, but for now I will reassert a strong bid without repeating my immediately previous offer.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid of about 0.961, conceding only on low-weight issueD to the opponent’s consistently signaled valueC. It is still early at t=0.18, and the opponent’s offers remain low for me and show little meaningful concession, so I should hold firm on my high-weight issues A, B, and C while avoiding an exact repeat of my last bid.

I am targeting a high-utility bid of about 0.877 because it is still early at t=0.20, so there is no need to move close to the opponent’s low offers yet. The opponent’s bids have stayed around the same low value to me and show no clear concession trend, so I am holding firm on the high-weight issues A and B while making a modest concession on issueC and keeping issueD at a tolerable lower-weight value.

At t=0.22 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a low-cost concession on issueD, which the opponent consistently sets to valueC. The opponent’s offers have not meaningfully improved for my utility and remain around the low-to-mid range, so I am holding firm on the high-weight issues while avoiding an exact repeat of my immediately previous bid.

I am targeting a high but not maximal utility bid (about 0.877), since it is still early at t=0.24 and the opponent’s offers remain low for me without a clear concession trend. The opponent consistently proposes issueD:valueC and avoids my preferred issueC:valueB, so I concede on issueD and moderately on issueC while holding firm on the high-weight issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still early at t=0.26 and the opponent’s offers have not materially improved my utility. The opponent appears to hold firm on issueD: valueC and often offers weak values for issueA/issueC, so I concede only the low-weight issueD while holding my high-weight priorities.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.81 while making a more meaningful concession than my prior bid because we are approaching the mid-game. The opponent has repeatedly proposed issueC: valueC and issueD: valueC, so I concede those while holding firm on my highest-value positions for issueA and issueB, where their offers remain far from my preferences.

At t=0.30 I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.81 while making a new concession compared with my ideal bid. The opponent has consistently offered issueD:valueC and lower values on issueA/issueC, so I concede on low-weight issueD and moderately on issueA while holding firm on my high-value issueB and issueC preferences.

I am targeting a utility around 0.79: still strong for me, but with meaningful concessions on issueB and issueD. At t=0.32 it is not yet late, so I should not move down to the opponent’s low-utility pattern, but their repeated offers suggest they may care about issueB=valueC and issueD=valueC, so I concede those while holding firm on my top values for issueA and issueC.

I am targeting a utility around 0.79, which is a meaningful concession from my maximum while still protecting the high-weight issues where I gain most. At t=0.34 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s low-utility pattern, which has stayed around 0.35–0.44 for me without clear concession. The opponent consistently offers issueD:valueC and issueB:valueB/valueC, so I concede on those while holding firm on issueA and issueC.

I targeted a utility of about 0.787, keeping my highest-value choices on the most important issues A and C while conceding on issueB and issueD where the opponent has repeatedly signaled interest. At t=0.36 there is some time pressure but not enough to make a large concession, especially since the opponent’s offers remain low-utility for me and show limited movement. Their bids consistently include issueD:valueC and often issueB:valueC, so I am accommodating those while holding firm on issueA:valueA and issueC:valueB.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.79 because we are not yet late, but I am continuing to concede on lower-cost issues rather than dropping on my most important preferred outcomes. The opponent’s offers remain clustered at low utility for me and show little real concession, with no messages to alter that assessment, so I will hold firm on issueA and issueC while accommodating their repeated issueB/issueD pattern.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.64 by conceding on issueA and issueB while holding firm on issueC, which is high value for me. The opponent has repeatedly offered issueA:valueD, issueB:valueB/valueC, issueC:valueC, issueD:valueC with little real concession, so I am not matching their low-utility bid yet but am moving toward values they seem to favor on some issues.

I am targeting a utility around 0.64: a meaningful mid-game concession from my ideal while still preserving value on the most important tradeoff, especially issueC. The opponent’s offers have been very stable and low for me, mostly around issueA=valueD, issueB=valueB/valueC, issueC=valueC, and issueD=valueC, so I am conceding on issueD and issueB and partially on issueA, but holding issueC at my top value.

I am targeting a relatively strong utility around 0.77 while making a meaningful tradeoff on issueA and issueD, which the opponent has repeatedly emphasized in their offers. Since we are still before the late phase at t=0.44, I should not drop to the opponent’s low-utility bids yet, but I can test a new compromise rather than repeat my previous bid.

I targeted a mid-utility compromise around 0.59, conceding on issueA and issueD where the opponent has repeatedly signaled preferences while holding issueC at my best value because it is highly weighted for me. At t=0.46 there is moderate time pressure, so I should move beyond near-optimal offers but not yet accept the opponent’s low-utility pattern. The opponent’s offers are fairly consistent rather than clearly improving, mostly using issueA valueC/valueD, issueB valueB/valueC, issueC valueC/valueD, and issueD valueC.

I targeted a utility around 0.59, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier high-utility bids but still well above the opponent’s low-value offers to me. At t=0.48 we are in the mid-game, so I am conceding on issueA, issueB, and issueD where the opponent has repeatedly signaled interest, while holding issueC at my best value because it is high-weight for me. The opponent has mostly held firm around issueA:valueD, issueB:valueB/valueC, issueC:valueC/valueD, issueD:valueC, with little improvement for my utility.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.59, conceding substantially on issueA and issueD while holding issueC at my top value because it is highly weighted. The opponent has consistently offered issueA:valueD, issueB:valueB/valueC, issueC:valueC/D, issueD:valueC, so I am matching most of their apparent priorities but not yet dropping issueC further at t=0.50.

I am targeting a utility around 0.592 by matching the opponent on issueA, issueB, and issueD while holding firm on issueC, which is one of my high-weight issues and where valueB is best for me. At t=0.52 there is meaningful time pressure but not enough to accept their low-utility pattern outright; their offers have mostly held steady around the same values rather than showing strong concession. Since their latest bid was issueA=valueD, issueB=valueC, issueC=valueC, issueD=valueC, this counter differs only on issueC and should be a focused compromise.

I targeted a mid-level utility around 0.59, continuing to concede on issueA, issueB, and issueD while holding issueC at valueB because it is high-importance for me. At t=0.54 there is some time pressure but not enough to drop to the opponent’s low-utility pattern; their repeated offers show little concession and consistently keep issueD at valueC and issueA around valueD/valueC.

I am targeting about 0.59 utility by keeping issueC at my top value while conceding on lower/replaceable points like issueD and issueB. At t=0.56 there is some time pressure, but the opponent’s offers have stayed low for me and mostly unchanged, so I will not yet drop to their issueC preference; I will avoid repeating my exact last bid by switching issueB to a value they often use and that is equal for me.

I am targeting a utility around 0.59 by conceding on issueA, issueB, and issueD while holding firm on issueC, which is one of my high-weight issues and where valueB is best for me. At t=0.58 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s repeated low-utility offers outright; their bids show they strongly prefer issueD:valueC and issueA:valueD, with no meaningful concession trend and no messages to weigh.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.59 at t=0.60, conceding substantially on issueA, issueB, and issueD while still holding issueC at my top value because it is a high-weight issue. The opponent has been very consistent around issueA=valueD, issueB=valueB/valueC, issueC=valueC, issueD=valueC, so I am matching most of their signaled preferences without dropping all the way to their latest offer yet.

I am targeting a moderate utility bid around 0.44, since time is past the midpoint and the opponent has shown little meaningful concession. Their repeated offers strongly signal they may accept issueA: valueD, issueB: valueC, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueC, so I concede on issueC while preserving a still-positive outcome above reservation.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.442, which is a meaningful concession but still above the opponent’s latest offer to me. At t=0.64 the opponent has mostly held firm around issueA=valueD/valueC, issueB=valueB/valueC, issueC=valueC/valueD, and issueD=valueC, with no messages to add credible information. I therefore concede on issueA and issueD and offer issueC=valueC instead of their worse valueD, using a combination they have previously signaled they may accept.
